unit.note: F0 gate green both workspaces. Server vitest 165 files / 1918 passed + 1 skipped (1919) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + tsc --noEmit clean + build green. Client vitest 203 files / 1972 passed. New F16 coverage lives in the scripts node:test suite (node --test scripts/kill-targets.test.js): 22 passed (9 pre-existing computeKillTargets + 13 new) - parseWindowsListenerPids returns ONLY exact-match LISTENING PIDs from a realistic netstat -ano dump; ignores ESTABLISHED browser sockets (9100); ignores :51730/:38470 prefix collisions (7777/8888); catches IPv6 TCP + TCPv6 listeners; ignores a UDP listener on the port (6000); empty/malformed tolerance; buildLsofCommand EXACT-string ordering (lsof -ti tcp:5173,3847 -sTCP:LISTEN - address bound to -i, state filter appended) + single-port + injection-proof coercion + empty-ports returns '' (kill nothing); dev-restart.js wiring guard (imports+invokes the fixed helpers, empty-cmd guard, no findstr / -p TCP / un-filtered lsof). Code-review round 2 fixed a HIGH lsof arg-ordering bug (the old order left -i addressless -> killed every macOS/Linux listener) - the exact-string test was proven RED against the broken order, green after. The scripts suite is separate from the vitest workspaces (root scripts/, run via node --test), so its 22 are reported here in the note rather than folded into the 3890 vitest count.

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes of the touches_io_boundary port-table surface (producer = OS netstat/lsof output -> file/pipe -> consumer parseWindowsListenerPids -> kill-target PID list). (1) Fixture round-trip unit probes (committed): a realistic netstat -ano dump with IPv4+IPv6 (TCP+TCPv6) real listeners, an ESTABLISHED browser socket, :51730/:38470 prefix collisions, and a UDP decoy -> parser returns EXACTLY {4200,4300}; 9100/7777/8888/6000 excluded. (2) LIVE Windows netstat -ano probe (throwaway): confirmed the real proto column is TCP for IPv6 [::] rows, `-p TCP` returns ZERO [::] rows (the IPv6 gap), and UDP rows are 4-token *:* (excluded by the <5 guard). This probe FOUND the IPv6 defect -> fixed (dropped -p TCP, startsWith('TCP')). (3) Re-run after the fix: fixture + wiring probes green, no further findings. Two consecutive clean rounds => asymptote reached, boundary calibrated.

e2e.note: No Playwright run. This iterate touches ONLY dev-tooling scripts at the repo root (scripts/dev-restart.js, scripts/kill-targets.js, scripts/kill-targets.test.js) - no client/src or server/src file, no DOM/route/component, no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface. detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false (nothing under client/**). F0.5 surface=none (see surface_verification); a browser smoke exercises nothing this change affects.

surface_verification.justification: This iterate changes only developer-tooling scripts at the repo root (scripts/dev-restart.js + scripts/kill-targets.js + scripts/kill-targets.test.js), which drive `npm run dev:fresh`. No file under client/src or server/src is touched - detect_frontend_changes.py classifies frontend by path (client/**) and reports has_frontend_changes=false, so there is genuinely no browser/DOM/route/Vite-config/proxy/WS/CORS surface to verify. Correctness is covered by the scripts node:test suite (21 passed, AC2 proven RED-first on pre-fix main: parseWindowsListenerPids/buildLsofCommand absent -> 7 TypeError failures, then green after) plus the empirical live-netstat calibration probe. F0.5 surface=none - no fabricated browser run.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - dev-restart kills ONLY the real listener PID: findPidsOnPorts now parses netstat structurally (proto startsWith TCP + state LISTENING + EXACT local-address port match) on Windows and runs `lsof -ti -sTCP:LISTEN tcp:<ports>` on POSIX; the over-matching scenarios (ESTABLISHED browser socket on :5173, :51730/:38470 prefix collisions) no longer reproduce, and IPv6 [::] listeners (missed by the old -p TCP) are now found.
behaviors.evidence: scripts/kill-targets.test.js: parseWindowsListenerPids over a realistic netstat -ano fixture returns EXACTLY {4200,4300} - browser ESTABLISHED (9100), :51730 (7777), :38470 (8888) and UDP-on-5173 (6000) all excluded; IPv6 TCP + TCPv6 rows resolve to the real PIDs; buildLsofCommand emits the EXACT canonical string `lsof -ti tcp:5173,3847 -sTCP:LISTEN` (address bound to -i, state filter appended - the round-2 HIGH fix) and returns '' for no valid ports. Live-netstat probe confirmed -p TCP drops all [::] rows (the IPv6 gap fixed by dropping it).

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression proof is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after
behaviors.evidence: RED captured on pre-fix main (kill-targets.js had no parser/builder): 7 new tests failed with `TypeError: parseWindowsListenerPids is not a function` / `buildLsofCommand is not a function`. GREEN after the fix. Round 2: the buildLsofCommand EXACT-string ordering + empty-ports tests were proven RED against the broken `lsof -ti -sTCP:LISTEN tcp:...` order (4 failed - the prior substring assert.match passed for it, hiding the bug), green after the reorder. Final: node --test scripts/kill-targets.test.js -> 22 passed / 0 failed (13 new).

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server + client suites + builds stay green (no invariant/ADR regressed)
behaviors.evidence: Server vitest 165 files / 1918 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + tsc + build green; client vitest 203 files / 1972 passed. The change is confined to root scripts/ (outside both workspaces), so neither suite is affected; oxlint clean; node --check clean on all three scripts. Read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact (script writes into no ~/.claude/projects/** or shipwright_run_config.json).

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected 3-file footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted
behaviors.evidence: Exact footprint: scripts/dev-restart.js (165 -> 179), scripts/kill-targets.js (43 -> 140), scripts/kill-targets.test.js (103 -> 292) - all < 300, no new bloat crossing, shipwright_bloat_baseline.json unchanged (no scripts/ entry). Serial #11, deps/blocks none, D01-D10 merged -> no live parallel collision.
